pi_items.source: Task description directly assigns the user the role of 'datacenter technician' performing a server memory upgrade.
Task description mandates creating a 'Server Memory' component category. setup_task.sh ensures no such category exists beforehand, and export_result.sh asserts its creation.
Task description explicitly lists Total Quantity 16 for Samsung 32GB.
Task description explicitly lists Minimum Quantity 4 for Samsung 32GB.
Task description explicitly lists Cost 89.99 for Samsung 32GB.
Task description states 'Check out 2 units of the Samsung 32GB component to the asset with tag SVR-UPG-001', which export_result.sh queries dynamically.
Task description explicitly lists Total Quantity 8 for Kingston 64GB.
Task description explicitly lists Minimum Quantity 2 for Kingston 64GB.
Task description explicitly lists Cost 199.50 for Kingston 64GB.
Task description states 'Check out 1 unit of the Kingston 64GB component to the asset with tag SVR-UPG-002', which export_result.sh queries dynamically.
setup_task.sh generates the 'Austin DC' location via API if it does not exist, and the task description instructs users to assign this location to the new components.
setup_task.sh injects a fictional server asset with this exact tag. The task description explicitly targets this tag for checkout.
setup_task.sh injects a fictional server asset with this exact tag. The task description explicitly targets this tag for checkout.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on synthetically generated data within Snipe-IT simulating a datacenter environment. Specifically, setup_task.sh injects fictional servers tagged 'SVR-UPG-001' and 'SVR-UPG-002', along with a target location 'Austin DC'. The expected component metadata strictly corresponds to the instructional parameters presented to the agent, including 16 Samsung 32GB units (min 4, $89.99), 8 Kingston 64GB units (min 2, $199.50), and checking out 2 and 1 unit(s) to the respective fictional servers.
